verses.text: Joab besieges Rabbah while 'David tarried at Jerusalem' — the quiet phrase that marks the season of his great fall. The crown is taken, the city subdued, the people set to labour. Idle ease, away from his post, was where the king's worst sin took root.

Joab besieges Rabbah while 'David tarried at Jerusalem' — the quiet phrase that marks the season of his great fall. The crown is taken, the city subdued, the people set to labour. Idle ease, away from his post, was where the king's worst sin took root.

Joab besieges Rabbah while 'David tarried at Jerusalem' — the quiet phrase that marks the season of his great fall. The crown is taken, the city subdued, the people set to labour. Idle ease, away from his post, was where the king's worst sin took root.

Wars follow with the giants of Gath, kin of Goliath, who 'fell by the hand of David, and by the hand of his servants.' The monsters that once terrified Israel are now routinely overcome. What the captain began against Goliath, his servants finish in God's strength.
